front: Poem: epic about the war between Eteocles and Polynices — Seven Against Thebes led by Adrastus
Poem: Tydeus has Melanippus's head ripped off and eats his brains
Poem: opens with Oedipus's curse; Fury Tisiphone answers prayer to bring strife between the brothers
Poem: Amphiaraus along with his chariot swallowed by the earth
Poem: divine horse Arion saves Adrastus (only survivor)
Poem: Hypsipyle leaves baby Opheltes to be crushed by a snake
Poem: unfinished epic about Achilles's childhood
Poem: he disguises as a woman at King Lycomedes's court on Scyros
Poem: Achilles conceives Neoptolemus with Deidamia while disguised as a woman on Scyros
Poem: 32 occasional poems in five books
Poem: opens with ekphrasis on equestrian statue of Domitian
Poem: 'Ode to Sleep' in Book 5 — 'some couple, bodies interlocked, must shut you from their ecstasy; so come to me'
Author: in Dante's Purgatorio, claims he secretly converted to Christianity after reading Virgil's fourth Eclogue
Author: envoi of his epic asks it to 'follow' the Aeneid 'at a distance and always adore their traces'
